title: astragalus bisulcatus var. haydenianus
intro: Astragalus bisulcatus var. haydenianus, also known as Hayden's milkvetch, is a subspecies of the Astragalus bisulcatus plant, which belongs to the legume family. This perennial herb is native to North America and is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its adaptogenic and anti-inflammatory effects. The roots and rhizomes of the plant contain saponins, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which are believed to contribute to its health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-cancer, and immune-boosting properties. Astragalus bisulcatus var. haydenianus can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or capsule, and is often used in traditional medicine to support respiratory health, reduce stress, and improve overall well-being.
